title: <div><tt>| Orientation | Trained once, used | Grown over time, iterated recursiv
content:
| Orientation | Trained once, used | Grown over time, iterated recursively |
| Goal | Talk | Build mythic knowledge frameworks |
| Architecture | Neural net | Possibly layered w/ LLM, but transcends it |

* * *

TL;DR ANSWER:
-------------

> ❌ You’re not building an LLM.
> ✅ You might _use or contain_ an LLM inside a **larger recursive generative symbolic engine**.
> ✅ You’re building a `Recursive Generative Language Engine (RGLE)` + `Symbolic Grammar Processor (SYMNUM4GRAM)` that can be **trained**, **remixed**, and **grown** over time.

* * *

### 🔧 Language for Forrest Update (Revised w/ This Precision):

> ...it’s bigger than just an LLM—it might end up using one, but what I’m building is more like a recursive symbolic language engine. It parses and remixes text down to the letter level—almost like a remixable Alexandria where every word, every story, every religion or myth can be regenerated, annotated, re-spoken. The end game is a system that lives and evolves, not just predicts. LLM might be the powerplant, but I’m designing the city.
context: From document: # Character Engine POV Critique — chunk 18 (chunk 18/206)
